1вҶ’# What Is Sequant
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’Sequant is workflow automation for Claude Code. It turns GitHub issues into
     4вҶ’merge-ready pull requests through structured phases and quality gates.
     5вҶ’
     6вҶ’You write issues. Sequant plans, builds, tests, and reviews them вҖ” each step
     7вҶ’with guardrails that catch problems before they reach your codebase.
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’---
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’## The Problem
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’Claude Code is remarkably capable. Give it a well-scoped issue and it can
    14вҶ’plan, implement, and test a feature in minutes. But capability alone doesn't
    15вҶ’guarantee consistency.
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’Without structure, AI-assisted development has a "results may vary" quality:
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’- A vague issue produces a vague implementation
    20вҶ’- There's no formal review step вҖ” code goes from "written" to "done"
    21вҶ’- Context accumulates over long sessions, drifting from the original goal
    22вҶ’- There's nothing tracking which issues are planned, in progress, or reviewed
    23вҶ’- Each session starts from scratch with no memory of prior work
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’These aren't flaws in Claude Code. They're the natural consequence of
    26вҶ’working without a process. Human developers solve this with methodologies,
    27вҶ’code review, CI/CD, and project management. AI-assisted development needs
    28вҶ’the same discipline.
    29вҶ’
    30вҶ’Sequant provides that discipline.
    31вҶ’
    32вҶ’---
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’## How It Works
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’Sequant installs slash commands into Claude Code that enforce a phased
    37вҶ’workflow. Each issue moves through plan, build, and review вҖ” with an
    38вҶ’isolated worktree keeping your main branch clean and a quality loop
    39вҶ’that catches problems before merge.
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’```text
    42вҶ’                         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    43вҶ’                         в”Ӯ GitHub Issue в”Ӯ
    44вҶ’                         в”Ӯ    #123      в”Ӯ
    45вҶ’                         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    46вҶ’                                в”Ӯ
    47вҶ’                         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    48вҶ’                         в”Ӯ    /spec     в”Ӯ  Runs in main repo.
    49вҶ’                         в”Ӯ     Plan     в”Ӯ  Reads issue, lints AC,
    50вҶ’                         в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ  posts plan to GitHub.
    51вҶ’                         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    52вҶ’                                в”Ӯ
    53вҶ’               в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    54вҶ’               в”Ӯ           WORKTREE                    в”Ӯ
    55вҶ’               в”Ӯ    isolated branch + dependencies     в”Ӯ
    56вҶ’               в”Ӯ                                       в”Ӯ
    57вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ӯ
    58вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ    /exec     в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    59вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ    Build     в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    60вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    61вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ Implement,   в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    62вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ commit, push,в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    63вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ create PR    в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ
    64вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ              в”Ӯ
    65вҶ’               в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
    66вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ              в”Ӯ
    67вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ     /qa      в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”җ          в”Ӯ
    68вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ    Review    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
    69вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
    70вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ Type safety, в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ /loop    в”Ӯ
    71вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ security,    в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ auto-fix в”Ӯ
    72вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ scope, tests в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ (up to   в”Ӯ
    73вҶ’               в”Ӯ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ   в”Ӯ  3x)     в”Ӯ
    74вҶ’               в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ          в”Ӯ
    75вҶ’               в”Ӯ           Pass? в”Җв”Җв”ҖNOв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ          в”Ӯ
    76вҶ’               в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
    77вҶ’               в”Ӯ               YES                     в”Ӯ
    78вҶ’               в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ                      в”Ӯ
    79вҶ’               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    80вҶ’                                в”Ӯ
    81вҶ’                         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–јв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    82вҶ’                         в”Ӯ    Merge     в”Ӯ  PR lands on main.
    83вҶ’                         в”Ӯ   + Clean    в”Ӯ  Worktree removed.
    84вҶ’                         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
    85вҶ’                                в”Ӯ
    86вҶ’                                в–ј
    87вҶ’                         Code on main
    88вҶ’```
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’Or run the full pipeline in one command:
    91вҶ’
    92вҶ’```text
    93вҶ’/fullsolve 123
    94вҶ’```
    95вҶ’
    96вҶ’### Each Phase Is a Fresh Conversation
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’This is a deliberate design choice. Phases don't share implicit context.
    99вҶ’The QA phase reviews the actual code diff вҖ” not a memory of having written
   100вҶ’it. The implementation phase reads the spec plan from the issue comment вҖ”
   101вҶ’not from conversation history.
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’Cross-phase context flows through explicit, inspectable channels:
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’| Channel | What it carries |
   106вҶ’| --- | --- |
   107вҶ’| **GitHub Comments** | Spec plans, QA verdicts, and phase markers posted to the issue thread |
   108вҶ’| **Git Diff** | What actually changed in the worktree |
   109вҶ’| **State File** (`.sequant/`) | Phase progress, AC status, and timestamps tracked locally per issue |
   110вҶ’| **Environment** | Issue number, worktree path, and base branch passed between phases |
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’This prevents context pollution, keeps phases composable, and makes the
   113вҶ’entire workflow auditable by reading issue comments.
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’---
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’## What Sequant Installs
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’Sequant is not a platform or a service. It's a set of files that live
   120вҶ’in your project and extend Claude Code's capabilities.
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’```text
   123вҶ’your-project/
   124вҶ’в”Ӯ
   125вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ .claude/
   126вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ skills/              в—„в”Җв”Җ 18 slash commands (markdown prompts)
   127вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ spec/SKILL.md         /spec, /exec, /qa, /fullsolve,
   128вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ exec/SKILL.md         /test, /testgen, /loop, /solve,
   129вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ qa/SKILL.md           /verify, /docs, /assess, /clean,
   130вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ ...                   /improve, /reflect, /merger,
   131вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ                             /security-review, /upstream
   132вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hooks/               в—„в”Җв”Җ 2 Claude Code hooks
   133вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ pre-tool.sh           Security guardrails, timing
   134вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ post-tool.sh          Quality observability, formatting
   135вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ settings.json        в—„в”Җв”Җ Hook configuration
   136вҶ’в”Ӯ
   137вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ .sequant/
   138вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ state.json           в—„в”Җв”Җ Workflow state per issue
   139вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ settings.json        в—„в”Җв”Җ Project configuration
   140вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ metrics.json         в—„в”Җв”Җ Aggregated run analytics
   141вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ logs/                в—„в”Җв”Җ Structured JSON run logs
   142вҶ’в”Ӯ
   143вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ scripts/dev/
   144вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ new-feature.sh       в—„в”Җв”Җ Create worktree from issue
   145вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cleanup-worktree.sh  в—„в”Җв”Җ Remove worktree after merge
   146вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ list-worktrees.sh    в—„в”Җв”Җ Show active worktrees
   147вҶ’в”Ӯ
   148вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ .sequant-manifest.json   в—„в”Җв”Җ Version tracking for updates
   149вҶ’```
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’Everything is local. No telemetry. No external services. Your code and
   152вҶ’workflow data stay in your repository.
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’---
   155вҶ’
   156вҶ’## What the Quality Gates Catch
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’The `/qa` phase isn't a rubber stamp. It runs structured checks against
   159вҶ’your code and renders one of four verdicts:
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’| Verdict | Meaning |
   162вҶ’| ------- | ------- |
   163вҶ’| **READY_FOR_MERGE** | All acceptance criteria met, quality checks pass |
   164вҶ’| **AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS** | Functional but has suggestions for improvement |
   165вҶ’| **NEEDS_VERIFICATION** | Requires manual testing or confirmation |
   166вҶ’| **AC_NOT_MET** | Acceptance criteria not satisfied вҖ” needs fixes |
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’The checks behind those verdicts:
   169вҶ’
   170вҶ’```text
   171вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   172вҶ’в”Ӯ                     /qa Quality Gates                   в”Ӯ
   173вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө
   174вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   175вҶ’в”Ӯ  AC Adherence в”Җв”Җв”Җ Does the code do what the issue asked в”Ӯ
   176вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   177вҶ’в”Ӯ  Type Safety в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Detects `any`, missing types,         в”Ӯ
   178вҶ’в”Ӯ                   unsafe casts                          в”Ӯ
   179вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   180вҶ’в”Ӯ  Security в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Semgrep static analysis, OWASP        в”Ӯ
   181вҶ’в”Ӯ                   checks, secret detection              в”Ӯ
   182вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   183вҶ’в”Ӯ  Scope в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Flags changes outside issue scope     в”Ӯ
   184вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   185вҶ’в”Ӯ  Test Coverage в”Җв”Җ File-by-file coverage, critical path  в”Ӯ
   186вҶ’в”Ӯ                   flagging, deleted test detection      в”Ӯ
   187вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   188вҶ’в”Ӯ  Build в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ Distinguishes regressions from        в”Ӯ
   189вҶ’в”Ӯ                   pre-existing failures                 в”Ӯ
   190вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   191вҶ’в”Ӯ  Anti-Patterns в”Җв”Җ N+1 queries, empty catch blocks,      в”Ӯ
   192вҶ’в”Ӯ                   over-mocking, stale dependencies      в”Ӯ
   193вҶ’в”Ӯ                                                         в”Ӯ
   194вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   195вҶ’```
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’When checks fail, `/loop` can automatically parse findings, apply fixes,
   198вҶ’and re-run вҖ” up to 3 iterations вҖ” without human intervention.
   199вҶ’
   200вҶ’---
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’## What Runs Underneath
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’The phases are the visible workflow. Underneath, four systems run
   205вҶ’continuously to keep everything safe, tracked, and connected:
   206вҶ’
   207вҶ’```text
   208вҶ’  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   209вҶ’  в”Ӯ  Skills   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   Hooks   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   State   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   GitHub   в”Ӯ
   210вҶ’  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Comments  в”Ӯ
   211вҶ’  в”Ӯ 18 slash  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Pre-tool: в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ .sequant/ в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
   212вҶ’  в”Ӯ commands  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ blocks    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ tracks    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Plans,     в”Ӯ
   213вҶ’  в”Ӯ that tell в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ secrets,  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ phase     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ verdicts,  в”Ӯ
   214вҶ’  в”Ӯ Claude    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ unsafe    в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ progress, в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ and phase  в”Ӯ
   215вҶ’  в”Ӯ Code what в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ commands  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ metrics,  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ markers    в”Ӯ
   216вҶ’  в”Ӯ to do in  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ run logs  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ that carry в”Ӯ
   217вҶ’  в”Ӯ each      в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Post-tool:в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ context    в”Ӯ
   218вҶ’  в”Ӯ phase     в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ formats,  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Updated   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ between    в”Ӯ
   219вҶ’  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ observes  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ at phase  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ sessions   в”Ӯ
   220вҶ’  в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ quality   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ boundariesв”Ӯ  в”Ӯ            в”Ӯ
   221вҶ’  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   222вҶ’```
   223вҶ’
   224вҶ’- **Skills** are markdown prompts вҖ” they tell Claude Code exactly what to
   225вҶ’  do in each phase. The intelligence lives in the prompts.
   226вҶ’- **Hooks** fire on every tool call. Pre-tool hooks block secrets,
   227вҶ’  destructive commands, and edits outside the worktree. Post-tool hooks
   228вҶ’  auto-format code and log quality signals.
   229вҶ’- **State** tracks phase progress locally in `.sequant/state.json`,
   230вҶ’  aggregates metrics across runs, and writes structured JSON logs.
   231вҶ’- **GitHub Comments** carry context between phases. The spec plan, QA
   232вҶ’  verdict, and phase markers are all posted to the issue thread вҖ”
   233вҶ’  making the workflow resumable across sessions and machines.
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’---
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’## Worktree Isolation
   238вҶ’
   239вҶ’The worktree in the diagram above isn't just a feature вҖ” it's the
   240вҶ’boundary between planning and execution. `/spec` runs in your main
   241вҶ’repo (no code changes). Everything inside the worktree вҖ” `/exec`,
   242вҶ’`/qa`, `/loop` вҖ” operates on an isolated branch with its own
   243вҶ’dependencies. Your main branch is never touched.
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’```text
   246вҶ’your-project/                     в—„в”Җв”Җ Main repo (stays on main)
   247вҶ’
   248вҶ’../worktrees/feature/
   249вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ 123-add-auth/             в—„в”Җв”Җ Issue #123 (own branch + deps)
   250вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ 124-fix-nav-bug/          в—„в”Җв”Җ Issue #124 (own branch + deps)
   251вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җ 125-update-api/           в—„в”Җв”Җ Issue #125 (own branch + deps)
   252вҶ’```
   253вҶ’
   254вҶ’Work on multiple issues in parallel. Discard failed experiments. When
   255вҶ’a PR merges, the worktree is cleaned up and the code lands on main.
   256вҶ’
   257вҶ’---
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’## Two Ways to Use It
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’### Interactive вҖ” Inside Claude Code
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’Type slash commands directly. Review plans before building. Step through
   264вҶ’at your own pace.
   265вҶ’
   266вҶ’```text
   267вҶ’/spec 123          Plan the implementation
   268вҶ’/exec 123          Build it
   269вҶ’/qa 123            Review it
   270вҶ’```
   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’### Headless вҖ” From the CLI
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’Run the full pipeline without the Claude Code UI. Batch multiple issues.
   275вҶ’Let it iterate overnight.
   276вҶ’
   277вҶ’```bash
   278вҶ’npx sequant run 123                    # Single issue, full pipeline
   279вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3                  # Three issues in parallel
   280вҶ’npx sequant run 123 --quality-loop     # Auto-fix on QA failure
   281вҶ’npx sequant run 123 --resume           # Pick up where you left off
   282вҶ’```
   283вҶ’
   284вҶ’---
   285вҶ’
   286вҶ’## Issues as Building Blocks
   287вҶ’
   288вҶ’The core idea behind Sequant is that a project is a collection of
   289вҶ’well-defined issues, and each issue follows the same lifecycle:
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’```text
   292вҶ’              в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   293вҶ’              в”Ӯ              Your Project                 в”Ӯ
   294вҶ’              в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
   295вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ
   296вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ #101  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ #102  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ #103  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
   297вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Auth  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Nav   в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ API   в”Ӯ  ...    в”Ӯ
   298вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ [done]в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ [done]в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ [wip] в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
   299вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ
   300вҶ’              в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
   301вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ӯ
   302вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ #104  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ #105  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ #106  в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
   303вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ Tests в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Perf  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ Docs  в”Ӯ  ...    в”Ӯ
   304вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ [plan]в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ [plan]в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ [plan]в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ
   305вҶ’              в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в”Ӯ
   306вҶ’              в”Ӯ                                           в”Ӯ
   307вҶ’              в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   308вҶ’```
   309вҶ’
   310вҶ’Each block passes through the same phases. Each block gets the same
   311вҶ’quality checks. The output is predictable because the process is
   312вҶ’consistent вҖ” not because every issue is simple, but because every
   313вҶ’issue is treated with the same rigor.
   314вҶ’
   315вҶ’Sequant doesn't make Claude Code less flexible. It makes the workflow
   316вҶ’around it more structured. You still get the full power of Claude Code
   317вҶ’inside each phase. Sequant just ensures that power is applied
   318вҶ’methodically вҖ” plan first, build in isolation, review before merging.
   319вҶ’
   320вҶ’---
   321вҶ’
   322вҶ’## Quick Start
   323вҶ’
   324вҶ’```bash
   325вҶ’npm install sequant
   326вҶ’npx sequant init
   327вҶ’npx sequant doctor
   328вҶ’```
   329вҶ’
   330вҶ’Then in Claude Code:
   331вҶ’
   332вҶ’```text
   333вҶ’/fullsolve 123
   334вҶ’```
   335вҶ’
   336вҶ’See the [Quickstart Guide](../guides/quickstart.md) for the full walkthrough.
   337вҶ’

1вҶ’# Run Command
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Quick Start:** Execute the Sequant workflow (`/spec` вҶ’ `/exec` вҶ’ `/qa`) for one or more GitHub issues with a single command. Use this to automate issue resolution through sequential AI phases.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Access
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’- **Command:** `npx sequant run <issues...> [options]`
     8вҶ’- **Requirements:**
     9вҶ’  - Sequant initialized (`sequant init`)
    10вҶ’  - Claude CLI installed and configured
    11вҶ’  - GitHub CLI authenticated (`gh auth login`)
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’## Usage
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’### Running a Single Issue
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’```bash
    18вҶ’npx sequant run 123
    19вҶ’```
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’Executes the default workflow phases (spec вҶ’ exec вҶ’ qa) for issue #123.
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### Running Multiple Issues
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’```bash
    26вҶ’npx sequant run 100 101 102
    27вҶ’```
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’Processes issues #100, #101, and #102. By default, issues continue processing even if one fails.
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’### Sequential Mode with Dependencies
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’```bash
    34вҶ’npx sequant run 100 101 102 --sequential
    35вҶ’```
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’Processes issues in order, stopping if any issue fails. Use this when later issues depend on earlier ones.
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’### Preview with Dry Run
    40вҶ’
    41вҶ’```bash
    42вҶ’npx sequant run 100 --dry-run
    43вҶ’```
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’Shows what would be executed without actually running any phases. Useful for verifying configuration.
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’## Options & Settings
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’| Option | Description | Default |
    50вҶ’|--------|-------------|---------|
    51вҶ’| `--phases <list>` | Comma-separated phases to run | `spec,exec,qa` |
    52вҶ’| `--sequential` | Run issues in order, stop on first failure (see [Execution Model](#execution-model)) | `false` |
    53вҶ’| `--chain` | Chain issues: each branches from previous (implies `--sequential`) | `false` |
    54вҶ’| `--qa-gate` | Wait for QA pass before starting next issue (requires `--chain`) | `false` |
    55вҶ’| `-d, --dry-run` | Preview without execution | `false` |
    56вҶ’| `-v, --verbose` | Show detailed output | `false` |
    57вҶ’| `--timeout <seconds>` | Timeout per phase | `1800` (30 min) |
    58вҶ’| `-q, --quality-loop` | Enable auto-retry on failures | `false` |
    59вҶ’| `--max-iterations <n>` | Max iterations for quality loop | `3` |
    60вҶ’| `--testgen` | Run testgen phase after spec | `false` |
    61вҶ’| `--batch "<issues>"` | Group issues to run together | - |
    62вҶ’| `--no-mcp` | Disable MCP servers for faster/cheaper runs | `false` |
    63вҶ’
    64вҶ’### Available Phases
    65вҶ’
    66вҶ’| Phase | Description |
    67вҶ’|-------|-------------|
    68вҶ’| `spec` | Planning and specification review |
    69вҶ’| `testgen` | Generate test stubs from spec |
    70вҶ’| `exec` | Implementation execution |
    71вҶ’| `test` | Run tests and verify |
    72вҶ’| `qa` | Quality review and approval |
    73вҶ’| `loop` | Quality iteration loop |
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’## Execution Model
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’Issues are always processed **one at a time** (serially). The `--sequential` flag controls **failure behavior**, not concurrency:
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’| Mode | Flag | Behavior on Failure |
    80вҶ’|------|------|---------------------|
    81вҶ’| Default | _(none)_ | Continue to next issue |
    82вҶ’| Sequential | `--sequential` | Stop immediately |
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’**Why not concurrent?** The Claude Agent SDK processes one agent session at a time. True concurrent execution (e.g., via listr2) is a potential future enhancement, but the current architecture runs issues serially regardless of the `--sequential` flag.
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’**What `--sequential` actually controls:**
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’```bash
    89вҶ’# Default: process all issues, continue if #101 fails
    90вҶ’npx sequant run 100 101 102
    91вҶ’#   вң“ #100 вҶ’ вң— #101 вҶ’ вң“ #102  (all attempted)
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’# Sequential: stop on first failure
    94вҶ’npx sequant run 100 101 102 --sequential
    95вҶ’#   вң“ #100 вҶ’ вң— #101  (stopped, #102 skipped)
    96вҶ’```
    97вҶ’
    98вҶ’> **Note:** The settings file and logs may show `"sequential": false` and `Mode: parallel`. This refers to the failure behavior described above вҖ” issues still run one at a time.
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’## Common Workflows
   101вҶ’
   102вҶ’### Standard Issue Resolution
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’Run the default workflow for a single issue:
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’```bash
   107вҶ’npx sequant run 42
   108вҶ’```
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’**What happens:**
   111вҶ’1. `/spec 42` - Reviews issue and creates implementation plan
   112вҶ’2. `/exec 42` - Implements the solution
   113вҶ’3. `/qa 42` - Reviews code and validates against acceptance criteria
   114вҶ’
   115вҶ’### Quick Fix (Skip Planning)
   116вҶ’
   117вҶ’For simple fixes where planning isn't needed:
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’```bash
   120вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --phases exec,qa
   121вҶ’```
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’### Full Workflow with Tests
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’Include test generation and execution:
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’```bash
   128вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --phases spec,testgen,exec,test,qa
   129вҶ’```
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’### Batch Processing
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’Process a sprint's worth of issues:
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’```bash
   136вҶ’npx sequant run 100 101 102 103 104 --sequential
   137вҶ’```
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’### Quality Loop Mode
   140вҶ’
   141вҶ’Enable automatic fix iterations when phases fail:
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’```bash
   144вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --quality-loop
   145вҶ’```
   146вҶ’
   147вҶ’**What happens:**
   148вҶ’1. Runs phases normally (spec вҶ’ exec вҶ’ qa)
   149вҶ’2. If a phase fails, runs `/loop` to fix issues
   150вҶ’3. Re-runs failed phases after fixes
   151вҶ’4. Iterates up to 3 times (configurable with `--max-iterations`)
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’This is useful for complex issues where initial implementation may need refinement.
   154вҶ’
   155вҶ’```bash
   156вҶ’# Quality loop with more iterations
   157вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --quality-loop --max-iterations 5
   158вҶ’```
   159вҶ’
   160вҶ’### Chain Mode
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’Run dependent issues where each branches from the previous:
   163вҶ’
   164вҶ’```bash
   165вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3 --sequential --chain
   166вҶ’```
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’**What happens:**
   169вҶ’
   170вҶ’1. Issue #1 branches from `origin/main`
   171вҶ’2. Issue #2 branches from `feature/1-xxx` (Issue #1's completed branch)
   172вҶ’3. Issue #3 branches from `feature/2-xxx` (Issue #2's completed branch)
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’```text
   175вҶ’origin/main
   176вҶ’    в””в”ҖвҶ’ feature/1-add-auth (Issue #1)
   177вҶ’            в””в”ҖвҶ’ feature/2-add-login-page (Issue #2)
   178вҶ’                    в””в”ҖвҶ’ feature/3-add-logout (Issue #3)
   179вҶ’```
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’**Checkpoint Commits:**
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’After each issue passes QA, a checkpoint commit is automatically created. This serves as a recovery point if later issues in the chain fail.
   184вҶ’
   185вҶ’**Requirements:**
   186вҶ’
   187вҶ’- `--chain` implies `--sequential` (issues must run in order)
   188вҶ’- Cannot be combined with `--batch` mode
   189вҶ’
   190вҶ’**Warnings:**
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’A warning is shown for chains longer than 5 issues. Long chains:
   193вҶ’- Increase merge complexity
   194вҶ’- Make code review more difficult
   195вҶ’- Are harder to recover from if failures occur
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’Consider breaking long chains into smaller batches.
   198вҶ’
   199вҶ’**Use Cases:**
   200вҶ’
   201вҶ’- Implementing features that build on each other
   202вҶ’- Multi-part refactoring where each step depends on the previous
   203вҶ’- Building a feature incrementally (auth вҶ’ login вҶ’ logout)
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’**Merging Chain PRs:**
   206вҶ’
   207вҶ’Option A: Sequential merge to main (recommended)
   208вҶ’```bash
   209вҶ’# Merge each PR in order, rebasing as needed
   210вҶ’gh pr merge 1 --squash --delete-branch
   211вҶ’# Update PR 2's base after 1 is merged
   212вҶ’gh pr merge 2 --squash --delete-branch
   213вҶ’gh pr merge 3 --squash --delete-branch
   214вҶ’```
   215вҶ’
   216вҶ’Option B: Single combined review
   217вҶ’- Review the final branch which contains all changes
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’### QA Gate Mode
   220вҶ’
   221вҶ’Add `--qa-gate` to pause the chain when QA fails, preventing downstream issues from building on potentially broken code:
   222вҶ’
   223вҶ’```bash
   224вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3 --sequential --chain --qa-gate
   225вҶ’```
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’**What happens:**
   228вҶ’
   229вҶ’1. Issue #1 runs through spec вҶ’ exec вҶ’ qa
   230вҶ’2. If QA passes: Continue to Issue #2
   231вҶ’3. If QA fails: Chain pauses with clear messaging
   232вҶ’
   233вҶ’**QA Gate Pause Output:**
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’```text
   236вҶ’  вҸёпёҸ  QA Gate
   237вҶ’     Issue #1 QA did not pass. Chain paused.
   238вҶ’     Fix QA issues and re-run, or run /loop to auto-fix.
   239вҶ’```
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’**State Tracking:**
   242вҶ’
   243вҶ’When QA gate pauses a chain, the issue status is set to `waiting_for_qa_gate`. Check status with:
   244вҶ’
   245вҶ’```bash
   246вҶ’sequant status --issues
   247вҶ’```
   248вҶ’
   249вҶ’**When to Use QA Gate:**
   250вҶ’
   251вҶ’- Complex chains where later issues depend heavily on earlier ones
   252вҶ’- When QA findings in early issues could invalidate later implementations
   253вҶ’- Production-critical chains where you want to ensure quality at each step
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’**When NOT to Use QA Gate:**
   256вҶ’
   257вҶ’- Simple, independent issues that don't build on each other
   258вҶ’- When you want maximum speed and can fix issues later
   259вҶ’- Chains where issues are mostly independent despite the branch structure
   260вҶ’
   261вҶ’**Recovery from QA Gate Pause:**
   262вҶ’
   263вҶ’Option A: Fix and re-run
   264вҶ’```bash
   265вҶ’# Fix the QA issues manually
   266вҶ’cd ../worktrees/feature/1-xxx
   267вҶ’# Make fixes...
   268вҶ’git commit -m "fix: address QA findings"
   269вҶ’
   270вҶ’# Re-run the full chain
   271вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3 --sequential --chain --qa-gate
   272вҶ’```
   273вҶ’
   274вҶ’Option B: Use /loop to auto-fix
   275вҶ’```bash
   276вҶ’# In the worktree, run loop to auto-fix
   277вҶ’/loop 1
   278вҶ’
   279вҶ’# Then re-run the chain
   280вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3 --sequential --chain --qa-gate
   281вҶ’```
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’**Combining with Quality Loop:**
   284вҶ’
   285вҶ’You can combine `--qa-gate` with `--quality-loop` for automatic retry:
   286вҶ’
   287вҶ’```bash
   288вҶ’npx sequant run 1 2 3 --sequential --chain --qa-gate --quality-loop
   289вҶ’```
   290вҶ’
   291вҶ’This will:
   292вҶ’1. Run each issue through phases
   293вҶ’2. If a phase fails, automatically retry with `/loop`
   294вҶ’3. If QA still fails after max iterations, pause the chain
   295вҶ’
   296вҶ’### CI/Scripting Mode
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’Run without colors for CI environments:
   299вҶ’
   300вҶ’```bash
   301вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --no-color
   302вҶ’```
   303вҶ’
   304вҶ’## Environment Variables
   305вҶ’
   306вҶ’| Variable | Description | Default |
   307вҶ’|----------|-------------|---------|
   308вҶ’| `PHASE_TIMEOUT` | Override default timeout (seconds) | `1800` |
   309вҶ’| `PHASES` | Override default phases | `spec,exec,qa` |
   310вҶ’| `SEQUANT_QUALITY_LOOP` | Enable quality loop | `false` |
   311вҶ’| `SEQUANT_MAX_ITERATIONS` | Max quality loop iterations | `3` |
   312вҶ’| `SEQUANT_SMART_TESTS` | Enable smart test detection | `true` |
   313вҶ’| `SEQUANT_TESTGEN` | Enable testgen phase | `false` |
   314вҶ’
   315вҶ’Example:
   316вҶ’```bash
   317вҶ’PHASE_TIMEOUT=3600 npx sequant run 42  # 1 hour timeout
   318вҶ’SEQUANT_QUALITY_LOOP=true npx sequant run 42  # Enable quality loop
   319вҶ’```
   320вҶ’
   321вҶ’## MCP Server Support
   322вҶ’
   323вҶ’`sequant run` supports MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for enhanced functionality in headless mode. When enabled, MCP servers configured in Claude Desktop are automatically passed to the Claude Agent SDK.
   324вҶ’
   325вҶ’### How It Works
   326вҶ’
   327вҶ’1. **Reads Claude Desktop config** from the platform-specific path:
   328вҶ’   - macOS: `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
   329вҶ’   - Windows: `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
   330вҶ’   - Linux: `~/.config/claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
   331вҶ’
   332вҶ’2. **Passes `mcpServers`** to the SDK `query()` call for each phase
   333вҶ’
   334вҶ’3. **Graceful degradation**: If the config doesn't exist or is invalid, runs without MCPs
   335вҶ’
   336вҶ’### Configuration
   337вҶ’
   338вҶ’| Option | Setting | Default | Description |
   339вҶ’|--------|---------|---------|-------------|
   340вҶ’| `--no-mcp` | - | - | Disable MCPs for faster/cheaper runs |
   341вҶ’| - | `run.mcp` | `true` | Enable MCP servers by default |
   342вҶ’
   343вҶ’**Priority:** CLI flag (`--no-mcp`) вҶ’ Settings (`run.mcp`) вҶ’ Default (`true`)
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’### Usage Examples
   346вҶ’
   347вҶ’```bash
   348вҶ’# Default: MCPs enabled (reads from Claude Desktop config)
   349вҶ’npx sequant run 42
   350вҶ’
   351вҶ’# Disable MCPs for faster execution
   352вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --no-mcp
   353вҶ’
   354вҶ’# Disable MCPs via settings
   355вҶ’# In .sequant/settings.json: { "run": { "mcp": false } }
   356вҶ’```
   357вҶ’
   358вҶ’### Checking MCP Availability
   359вҶ’
   360вҶ’Run `sequant doctor` to verify MCP availability for headless mode:
   361вҶ’
   362вҶ’```bash
   363вҶ’sequant doctor
   364вҶ’```
   365вҶ’
   366вҶ’Look for the "MCP Servers (headless)" check:
   367вҶ’- вң“ **Pass**: MCPs available for `sequant run`
   368вҶ’- вҡ  **Warn**: No Claude Desktop config found or empty `mcpServers`
   369вҶ’
   370вҶ’### Supported MCPs
   371вҶ’
   372вҶ’MCPs that enhance Sequant skills in headless mode:
   373вҶ’
   374вҶ’| MCP | Skills Enhanced | Purpose |
   375вҶ’|-----|-----------------|---------|
   376вҶ’| Context7 | `/exec`, `/fullsolve` | External library documentation lookup |
   377вҶ’| Sequential Thinking | `/fullsolve` | Complex multi-step reasoning |
   378вҶ’| Chrome DevTools | `/test`, `/testgen`, `/loop` | Browser automation for UI testing |
   379вҶ’
   380вҶ’### Adding MCPs for Headless Mode
   381вҶ’
   382вҶ’To add MCP servers for use with `sequant run`, edit the Claude Desktop config file directly.
   383вҶ’
   384вҶ’**1. Locate your config file:**
   385вҶ’
   386вҶ’| Platform | Path |
   387вҶ’|----------|------|
   388вҶ’| macOS | `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` |
   389вҶ’| Windows | `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json` |
   390вҶ’| Linux | `~/.config/claude/claude_desktop_config.json` |
   391вҶ’
   392вҶ’**2. Add MCPs to the `mcpServers` object:**
   393вҶ’
   394вҶ’```json
   395вҶ’{
   396вҶ’  "mcpServers": {
   397вҶ’    "context7": {
   398вҶ’      "command": "npx",
   399вҶ’      "args": ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp"]
   400вҶ’    },
   401вҶ’    "chrome-devtools": {
   402вҶ’      "command": "npx",
   403вҶ’      "args": ["-y", "chrome-devtools-mcp@latest"]
   404вҶ’    },
   405вҶ’    "sequential-thinking": {
   406вҶ’      "command": "npx",
   407вҶ’      "args": ["-y", "@modelcontextprotocol/server-sequential-thinking"]
   408вҶ’    }
   409вҶ’  }
   410вҶ’}
   411вҶ’```
   412вҶ’
   413вҶ’**3. Config format:**
   414вҶ’
   415вҶ’```json
   416вҶ’{
   417вҶ’  "mcpServers": {
   418вҶ’    "<server-name>": {
   419вҶ’      "command": "npx",           // Command to run
   420вҶ’      "args": ["-y", "<package>"], // Arguments (use -y for auto-install)
   421вҶ’      "env": {                     // Optional: environment variables
   422вҶ’        "API_KEY": "..."
   423вҶ’      }
   424вҶ’    }
   425вҶ’  }
   426вҶ’}
   427вҶ’```
   428вҶ’
   429вҶ’**4. Verify configuration:**
   430вҶ’
   431вҶ’```bash
   432вҶ’sequant doctor
   433вҶ’```
   434вҶ’
   435вҶ’Look for:
   436вҶ’- вң“ `MCP Servers: All optional MCPs configured`
   437вҶ’- вң“ `MCP Servers (headless): Available for sequant run (N servers configured)`
   438вҶ’
   439вҶ’**Note:** Changes to Claude Desktop config require restarting Claude Desktop (for interactive use) but take effect immediately for `sequant run` (headless mode).
   440вҶ’
   441вҶ’### When to Disable MCPs
   442вҶ’
   443вҶ’Use `--no-mcp` when:
   444вҶ’- Running on a system without Claude Desktop installed
   445вҶ’- Optimizing for cost (MCPs add token overhead)
   446вҶ’- Running simple issues that don't need external documentation
   447вҶ’- Debugging to isolate MCP-related issues
   448вҶ’
   449вҶ’## Settings File
   450вҶ’
   451вҶ’You can configure defaults in `.sequant/settings.json`:
   452вҶ’
   453вҶ’```json
   454вҶ’{
   455вҶ’  "version": "1.0",
   456вҶ’  "run": {
   457вҶ’    "logJson": true,
   458вҶ’    "logPath": ".sequant/logs",
   459вҶ’    "autoDetectPhases": true,
   460вҶ’    "timeout": 1800,
   461вҶ’    "sequential": false,
   462вҶ’    "qualityLoop": false,
   463вҶ’    "maxIterations": 3,
   464вҶ’    "smartTests": true,
   465вҶ’    "mcp": true
   466вҶ’  }
   467вҶ’}
   468вҶ’```
   469вҶ’
   470вҶ’Settings hierarchy (highest priority wins):
   471вҶ’1. CLI flags (`--quality-loop`)
   472вҶ’2. Environment variables (`SEQUANT_QUALITY_LOOP`)
   473вҶ’3. Project settings (`.sequant/settings.json`)
   474вҶ’4. Package defaults
   475вҶ’

   521вҶ’## Worktree Isolation
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’By default, `sequant run` creates isolated git worktrees for each issue. This ensures:
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’- **Clean separation**: Each issue's changes are isolated from others
   526вҶ’- **Parallel safety**: Multiple issues can be worked on simultaneously
   527вҶ’- **Easy cleanup**: Worktrees can be removed without affecting other work
   528вҶ’
   529вҶ’### Fresh Baseline
   530вҶ’
   531вҶ’When creating a new worktree, Sequant:
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’1. **Fetches latest main**: Runs `git fetch origin main`
   534вҶ’2. **Branches from origin/main**: Creates the branch from `origin/main`
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’This guarantees every new issue starts from the latest remote state.
   537вҶ’
   538вҶ’### Worktree Location
   539вҶ’
   540вҶ’Worktrees are created in a `worktrees/` directory alongside your repository:
   541вҶ’
   542вҶ’```text
   543вҶ’parent-directory/
   544вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ your-repo/           # Main repository
   545вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ worktrees/
   546вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ feature/123-add-login/
   547вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җ feature/124-fix-bug/
   548вҶ’```
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’### Reusing Worktrees
   551вҶ’
   552вҶ’If a worktree already exists for an issue's branch, Sequant reuses it.
   553вҶ’This preserves any in-progress work.
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’In **chain mode**, existing worktrees are automatically rebased onto the previous chain link. If a rebase conflict occurs, the rebase is aborted and the worktree continues in its original state with a warning.
   556вҶ’
   557вҶ’### Phase Isolation
   558вҶ’
   559вҶ’Not all phases run in the worktree:
   560вҶ’
   561вҶ’| Phase  | Location  | Reason                           |
   562вҶ’| ------ | --------- | -------------------------------- |
   563вҶ’| `spec` | Main repo | Planning only, no code changes   |
   564вҶ’| `exec` | Worktree  | Implementation happens here      |
   565вҶ’| `test` | Worktree  | Tests run against implementation |
   566вҶ’| `qa`   | Worktree  | Review happens in context        |
   567вҶ’
   568вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   569вҶ’
   570вҶ’### "Sequant is not initialized"
   571вҶ’
   572вҶ’**Symptoms:** Error message says Sequant is not initialized
   573вҶ’
   574вҶ’**Solution:** Run `sequant init` in your project root first:
   575вҶ’```bash
   576вҶ’sequant init
   577вҶ’```
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’### Phase timeout
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’**Symptoms:** Phase fails with "Timeout after 1800s"
   582вҶ’
   583вҶ’**Solution:** Increase the timeout:
   584вҶ’```bash
   585вҶ’npx sequant run 42 --timeout 3600  # 1 hour
   586вҶ’```
   587вҶ’
   588вҶ’### Claude CLI not found
   589вҶ’
   590вҶ’**Symptoms:** Error about `claude` command not found
   591вҶ’
   592вҶ’**Solution:** Install and configure Claude CLI:
   593вҶ’```bash
   594вҶ’npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
   595вҶ’claude --version
   596вҶ’```
   597вҶ’
   598вҶ’### GitHub authentication
   599вҶ’
   600вҶ’**Symptoms:** Issues can't be fetched or commented on
   601вҶ’
   602вҶ’**Solution:** Authenticate GitHub CLI:
   603вҶ’```bash
   604вҶ’gh auth login
   605вҶ’gh auth status
   606вҶ’```
   607вҶ’

    60вҶ’### Why Sequant Exists
    61вҶ’
    62вҶ’AI coding assistants are powerful but chaotic. Without structure, you get:
    63вҶ’- Half-finished features scattered across branches
    64вҶ’- Acceptance criteria ignored or misunderstood
    65вҶ’- No clear definition of "done"
    66вҶ’- Security issues slipping through
    67вҶ’
    68вҶ’Sequant brings **discipline without friction**:
    69вҶ’- **Phases** ensure nothing gets skipped
    70вҶ’- **Worktrees** keep each issue isolated
    71вҶ’- **Quality gates** catch problems before merge
    72вҶ’- **Local state** tracks what's done and what's not
    73вҶ’
    74вҶ’### Phase Isolation by Design
    75вҶ’
    76вҶ’Each phase runs as a **fresh conversation** вҖ” no implicit memory carried forward. This is intentional:
    77вҶ’
    78вҶ’- **No context pollution** вҖ” stale planning notes don't crowd out implementation context
    79вҶ’- **Honest review** вҖ” `/qa` evaluates the actual diff, not the implementer's memory of it
    80вҶ’- **Composable phases** вҖ” run `/qa` alone, re-run `/exec` after failure, skip phases freely
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’Cross-phase context flows through **explicit channels**:
    83вҶ’`state.json` (phase progress, AC status), GitHub issue
    84вҶ’comments (spec plans, QA verdicts), git diff (what actually
    85вҶ’changed), and environment variables (orchestration context).
    86вҶ’These are inspectable, scoped per issue, and don't accumulate
    87вҶ’noise.
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’---
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’## Skill Commands
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’Sequant provides **18 slash commands** organized by purpose. Run them inside Claude Code.
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’### Core Workflow
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’The four pillars of the Sequant workflow:
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’| Command | Purpose | What It Does |
   100вҶ’|---------|---------|--------------|
   101вҶ’| `/spec` | **Plan** | Extracts AC, creates implementation plans, posts to GitHub |
   102вҶ’| `/exec` | **Build** | Creates worktrees, implements incrementally, creates PRs |
   103вҶ’| `/test` | **Verify** | Browser automation via Chrome DevTools, screenshot evidence |
   104вҶ’| `/qa` | **Review** | Validates AC, runs quality checks, renders merge verdicts |
   105вҶ’
   106вҶ’**`/spec` details:**
   107вҶ’- Lints AC for vague/unmeasurable terms
   108вҶ’- Requires explicit verification methods (Unit/Integration/Browser/Manual)
   109вҶ’- Feature Quality Planning (6 dimensions: completeness, error handling,
   110вҶ’  code quality, test coverage, best practices, polish)
   111вҶ’- Scope Assessment with verdicts: `SCOPE_OK` / `SCOPE_WARNING` /
   112вҶ’  `SCOPE_SPLIT_RECOMMENDED`
   113вҶ’- Content analysis (title/body patterns вҶ’ phase recommendations)
   114вҶ’- Testgen auto-detection, conflict detection, Derived ACs
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’**`/exec` details:**
   117вҶ’- Pre-PR AC verification (checks each AC before PR creation)
   118вҶ’- Shell script quality checks
   119вҶ’- Mandatory prompt templates for parallel sub-agents
   120вҶ’
   121вҶ’**`/test` details:**
   122вҶ’- Coverage analysis (warns when changed files lack tests)
   123вҶ’- Graceful fallback to manual checklists
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’**`/qa` details:**
   126вҶ’- Type safety, security scans, Semgrep static analysis
   127вҶ’- Test tautology detection (flags tests that don't call production code)
   128вҶ’- Call-site review (verifies new functions are called correctly, not just implemented correctly)
   129вҶ’- CI status awareness, build verification against main
   130вҶ’- Caches expensive checks (`--no-cache` to force fresh)
   131вҶ’- Verdicts: `READY_FOR_MERGE`, `AC_NOT_MET`,
   132вҶ’  `NEEDS_VERIFICATION`, `SECURITY_CONCERN`
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’### Automation Commands
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’For when you want to go hands-off:
   137вҶ’
   138вҶ’| Command | Purpose | What It Does |
   139вҶ’|---------|---------|--------------|
   140вҶ’| `/fullsolve` | **End-to-End** | Orchestrates specвҶ’execвҶ’testвҶ’qa with auto-fix loops |
   141вҶ’| `/solve` | **Advisor** | Recommends optimal workflow, outputs CLI commands |
   142вҶ’| `/loop` | **Quality Loop** | Parses findings, applies fixes, re-validates |
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’### Testing & Verification
   145вҶ’

   147вҶ’|---------|---------|--------------|

   149вҶ’| `/verify` | **CLI Verification** | Runs commands, captures output, posts evidence |
   150вҶ’| `/docs` | **Documentation** | Auto-detects doc type, generates operational docs |

   152вҶ’### Analysis & Utilities
   153вҶ’
   154вҶ’| Command | Purpose | What It Does |
   155вҶ’|---------|---------|--------------|

   161вҶ’| `/merger` | **Integration** | Multi-issue merge with conflict detection |
   162вҶ’| `/upstream` | **Tracking** | Monitors Claude Code releases, creates issues |

   171вҶ’
   172вҶ’## CLI Commands
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’The `sequant` CLI provides **10 commands** for project management.
   175вҶ’
   176вҶ’```bash
   177вҶ’npm install -g sequant   # Install globally
   178вҶ’npx sequant              # Or run via npx
   179вҶ’```
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’| Command | Description |
   182вҶ’|---------|-------------|
   183вҶ’| `sequant init` | Initialize Sequant in a project (copies templates, creates `.claude/` and `.sequant/`) |
   184вҶ’| `sequant doctor` | Check installation health вҖ” prerequisites, closed-issue verification, config validation |
   185вҶ’| `sequant run <issues>` | Execute workflow (`-q` quality loop, `--chain`, `--resume`, `--force`) |
   186вҶ’| `sequant status` | Show version, config, tracked issues with cleanup options |
   187вҶ’| `sequant update` | Update skill templates to latest versions |
   188вҶ’| `sequant state` | Manage workflow state (`init`, `rebuild`, `clean`) |
   189вҶ’| `sequant stats` | View local workflow analytics вҖ” success rates, timing, phase distribution |
   190вҶ’| `sequant logs` | View and manage log files with rotation |
   191вҶ’| `sequant merge` | Batch-level integration QA вҖ” verify feature branches before merging (`--check`, `--scan`, `--post`) |
   192вҶ’| `sequant dashboard` | Launch real-time workflow dashboard (Hono-powered) |
   193вҶ’
   194вҶ’### Quick Examples
   195вҶ’
   196вҶ’```bash
   197вҶ’# Initialize a new project
   198вҶ’sequant init
   199вҶ’
   200вҶ’# Check everything is working
   201вҶ’sequant doctor
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’# Run full workflow for issue #42
   204вҶ’sequant run 42
   205вҶ’
   206вҶ’# Run with quality loop (auto-retry on failures)
   207вҶ’sequant run 42 -q
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’# View your success rates
   210вҶ’sequant stats
   211вҶ’```
   212вҶ’

   215вҶ’## Core Libraries
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’The engine room lives in `src/lib/`. These modules power everything.
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’### Primary Libraries
   220вҶ’
   221вҶ’| Module | Purpose |
   222вҶ’|--------|---------|
   223вҶ’| `stacks.ts` | Detects project type and package manager, provides build/test commands |
   224вҶ’| `semgrep.ts` | Stack-aware Semgrep static analysis with custom rules support |
   225вҶ’| `templates.ts` | Copies skill templates to `.claude/`, handles variable substitution |
   226вҶ’| `manifest.ts` | Tracks installed skills and versions in `.sequant-manifest.json` |
   227вҶ’| `settings.ts` | Reads/writes `.sequant/settings.json` for persistent configuration (deep merge for nested objects) |
   228вҶ’| `config.ts` | Core configuration file operations |
   229вҶ’| `fs.ts` | Async file helpers (`exists`, `read`, `write`, `ensureDir`) |
   230вҶ’| `system.ts` | Validates prerequisites (`gh` CLI, `jq`), MCP server configuration |
   231вҶ’| `tty.ts` | Detects interactive vs non-interactive mode |
   232вҶ’| `wizard.ts` | Guides users through dependency installation |
   233вҶ’| `shutdown.ts` | Signal handling for graceful shutdown |
   234вҶ’| `version-check.ts` | Checks for updates, warns on stale local installs |
   235вҶ’| `cli-ui.ts` | Centralized CLI UI (spinners, boxes, tables, colors, ASCII branding) with graceful degradation |
   236вҶ’| `phase-spinner.ts` | Animated phase spinner with elapsed time and TTY fallback |
   237вҶ’| `ac-parser.ts` | Extracts acceptance criteria from markdown (AC-1, B2, etc.) |
   238вҶ’| `ac-linter.ts` | Flags vague, unmeasurable, incomplete, or open-ended AC before implementation |
   239вҶ’| `content-analyzer.ts` | Analyzes issue title/body for phase-relevant keywords (UI, security, complexity patterns) |
   240вҶ’| `phase-signal.ts` | Phase signal types and priority-based merging (Labels > Solve > Title > Body) |
   241вҶ’| `solve-comment-parser.ts` | Detects and parses `/solve` workflow recommendations from issue comments |
   242вҶ’| `project-name.ts` | Auto-detects project name from package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, go.mod, git remote |

   266вҶ’## Stack Support
   267вҶ’
   268вҶ’Sequant auto-detects your project type and configures itself.
   269вҶ’Each stack injects testing, linting, and build notes into your
   270вҶ’constitution template.
   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’| Stack | Detection | Build Command | Test Command |
   273вҶ’|-------|-----------|---------------|--------------|
   274вҶ’| **Next.js** | `next.config.*`, `next` dep | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   275вҶ’| **Astro** | `astro.config.*`, `astro` dep | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   276вҶ’| **SvelteKit** | `svelte.config.*`, `@sveltejs/kit` dep | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   277вҶ’| **Remix** | `remix.config.*`, `@remix-run/react` dep | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   278вҶ’| **Nuxt** | `nuxt.config.*`, `nuxt` dep | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   279вҶ’| **Rust** | `Cargo.toml` | `cargo build --release` | `cargo test` |
   280вҶ’| **Python** | `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, `requirements.txt` | `python -m build` | `pytest` |
   281вҶ’| **Go** | `go.mod` | `go build ./...` | `go test ./...` |
   282вҶ’| **Generic** | `package.json` (fallback) | `npm run build` | `npm test` |
   283вҶ’
   284вҶ’Stack-specific constitution notes include:
   285вҶ’- **Testing:** Frameworks, patterns, test file locations
   286вҶ’- **Linting:** Tools, commands, configuration
   287вҶ’- **Build:** Output directories, commands, deployment notes

   419вҶ’## Architecture Deep Dive
   420вҶ’
   421вҶ’### Directory Structure
   422вҶ’
   423вҶ’```
   424вҶ’sequant/
   425вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ bin/cli.ts              # CLI entry point
   426вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
   427вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ commands/           # CLI command implementations
   428вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ lib/                # Shared utilities
   429вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ workflow/       # State, metrics, logging
   430вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ index.ts            # Public exports
   431вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ skills/                 # Live skill files (16)
   432вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ templates/              # Distributable templates
   433вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ skills/             # Skill templates
   434вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hooks/              # Pre/post tool hooks
   435вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ memory/             # Constitution template
   436вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ scripts/            # Helper scripts
   437вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ stacks/                 # Stack configurations
   438вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ hooks/                  # Tool hooks
   439вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ scripts/                # Shell scripts
   440вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ dashboard/              # Real-time dashboard
   441вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ vscode-extension/       # VS Code extension
   442вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ docs/                   # Documentation
   443вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ .claude-plugin/         # Plugin marketplace config
   444вҶ’```
   445вҶ’
   446вҶ’### Integration Points
   447вҶ’
   448вҶ’**Claude Agent SDK**
   449вҶ’- Skills are executed through the SDK
   450вҶ’- Proper tool permissions per skill
   451вҶ’- Graceful error handling
   452вҶ’
   453вҶ’**GitHub CLI (`gh`)**
   454вҶ’- Issue fetching and parsing
   455вҶ’- PR creation and management
   456вҶ’- Comment posting
   457вҶ’- Label management
   458вҶ’
   459вҶ’**Git Worktrees**
   460вҶ’- One worktree per issue
   461вҶ’- Clean isolation
   462вҶ’- Easy cleanup
   463вҶ’
   464вҶ’**Optional MCPs**
   465вҶ’- `chrome-devtools` вҖ” Browser testing with `/test`
   466вҶ’- `sequential-thinking` вҖ” Complex reasoning
   467вҶ’- `context7` вҖ” Library documentation lookup
   468вҶ’
   469вҶ’### Data Flow
   470вҶ’
   471вҶ’```
   472вҶ’Issue #123 в”Җв”Җв–ә /spec в”Җв”Җв–ә Plan Posted в”Җв”Җв–ә User Approves
   473вҶ’                              в”Ӯ
   474вҶ’                              в–ј
   475вҶ’              /exec в”Җв”Җв–ә Worktree Created в”Җв”Җв–ә Code Written в”Җв”Җв–ә PR Created
   476вҶ’                              в”Ӯ
   477вҶ’                              в–ј
   478вҶ’              /test в”Җв”Җв–ә Tests Run в”Җв”Җв–ә Evidence Captured
   479вҶ’                              в”Ӯ
   480вҶ’                              в–ј
   481вҶ’               /qa в”Җв”Җв–ә Quality Checks в”Җв”Җв–ә Verdict Rendered
   482вҶ’                              в”Ӯ
   483вҶ’                        в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   484вҶ’                        в–ј           в–ј
   485вҶ’                 READY_FOR_MERGE    NEEDS_WORK
   486вҶ’                        в”Ӯ           в”Ӯ
   487вҶ’                        в–ј           в–ј
   488вҶ’                    Merge PR    /loop в”Җв”Җв–ә Fix в”Җв”Җв–ә Re-QA
   489вҶ’```

   492вҶ’
   493вҶ’## Hooks System
   494вҶ’
   495вҶ’The **pre-tool hook** (`templates/hooks/pre-tool.sh`) is a
   496вҶ’450+ line security and quality enforcement system. It intercepts
   497вҶ’every tool call before execution.
   498вҶ’
   499вҶ’### Security Guardrails
   500вҶ’
   501вҶ’Blocks catastrophic commands before they run:
   502вҶ’
   503вҶ’| Threat | What's Blocked | Why |
   504вҶ’|--------|----------------|-----|
   505вҶ’| **Credential Reading** | Reading SSH keys, env files | Prevents secret exfiltration |
   506вҶ’| **System Destruction** | Recursive deletes on root/home | Prevents accidental destruction |
   507вҶ’| **Force Push** | Git force push commands | Protects shared history |
   508вҶ’| **Deployment** | Production deployment commands | Automation shouldn't deploy |
   509вҶ’| **CI Triggers** | Workflow dispatch commands | Prevents automation loops |
   510вҶ’| **Environment Dump** | Bare environment dump commands | Blocks credential harvesting |
   511вҶ’
   512вҶ’### Secret Detection
   513вҶ’
   514вҶ’Scans staged commits for hardcoded secrets:
   515вҶ’
   516вҶ’- OpenAI API keys (`sk-...`)
   517вҶ’- Stripe live keys (`sk_live_...`)
   518вҶ’- AWS Access Keys (`AKIA...`)
   519вҶ’- GitHub Personal Tokens (`ghp_...`)
   520вҶ’- Slack Bot Tokens (`xoxb-...`)
   521вҶ’- Google API Keys (`AIza...`)
   522вҶ’
   523вҶ’### Sensitive File Detection
   524вҶ’
   525вҶ’Blocks commits containing:
   526вҶ’- Environment files (`.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.production`)
   527вҶ’- Credential JSON files
   528вҶ’- Key and certificate files (`.pem`, `.key`)
   529вҶ’- SSH private keys
   530вҶ’
   531вҶ’### Conventional Commits Enforcement
   532вҶ’
   533вҶ’Validates commit messages follow the format:
   534вҶ’
   535вҶ’```text
   536вҶ’type(scope): description
   537вҶ’
   538вҶ’Types: feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|test|chore|ci|build|perf
   539вҶ’```
   540вҶ’
   541вҶ’Provides helpful suggestions for common scenarios:
   542вҶ’- Merge commits: Suggests `chore: merge main into feature branch` format
   543вҶ’
   544вҶ’### Worktree Path Enforcement
   545вҶ’
   546вҶ’When `SEQUANT_WORKTREE` is set (by `sequant run`), blocks
   547вҶ’file edits outside the designated worktree. Prevents accidental
   548вҶ’edits to the main repo during feature work.
   549вҶ’
   550вҶ’### File Locking for Parallel Agents
   551вҶ’
   552вҶ’Uses `lockf` (macOS) or `flock` (Linux) to prevent concurrent
   553вҶ’edits to the same file when multiple agents run in parallel.
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’### Git Reset Protection
   556вҶ’
   557вҶ’Blocks hard resets when there's local work that would be lost:
   558вҶ’- Unpushed commits on main/master
   559вҶ’- Uncommitted changes (staged or unstaged)
   560вҶ’- Unfinished merge in progress
   561вҶ’
   562вҶ’### Pre-Merge Worktree Cleanup
   563вҶ’
   564вҶ’Automatically removes worktrees before PR merge to prevent branch deletion failures (worktrees lock their branches).
   565вҶ’
   566вҶ’### Timing Instrumentation
   567вҶ’
   568вҶ’Logs tool execution times to `/tmp/claude-timing.log` for performance analysis.
   569вҶ’
   570вҶ’### Rollback Mechanism
   571вҶ’
   572вҶ’Set `CLAUDE_HOOKS_DISABLED=true` to bypass all hook logic when needed.
   573вҶ’

   596вҶ’| `sequant.openPR` | PR | View pull request |

   604вҶ’
   605вҶ’## Real-Time Dashboard
   606вҶ’
   607вҶ’A **1000+ line web UI** for visualizing workflow state in real-time.
   608вҶ’
   609вҶ’### Tech Stack
   610вҶ’
   611вҶ’| Component | Technology |
   612вҶ’|-----------|------------|
   613вҶ’| **Server** | Hono (lightweight, fast) |
   614вҶ’| **Interactivity** | htmx (HTML-first) |
   615вҶ’| **Styling** | Pico CSS (classless) |
   616вҶ’| **Live Updates** | Server-Sent Events (SSE) |
   617вҶ’| **File Watching** | Chokidar |
   618вҶ’
   619вҶ’### Launch
   620вҶ’
   621вҶ’```bash
   622вҶ’sequant dashboard           # Opens http://localhost:3456
   623вҶ’sequant dashboard --port 8080
   624вҶ’sequant dashboard --no-open # Don't auto-open browser
   625вҶ’```
   626вҶ’
   627вҶ’### Features
   628вҶ’
   629вҶ’**Issue Cards**
   630вҶ’- Status badges (in_progress, ready_for_merge, blocked, etc.)
   631вҶ’- Phase indicators with animated pulses for active phase
   632вҶ’- Tooltips showing duration and error messages
   633вҶ’- PR links and worktree paths
   634вҶ’- Branch names with copy-to-clipboard
   635вҶ’
   636вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria Tracking**
   637вҶ’- Expandable AC sections per issue
   638вҶ’- Status icons (met, not_met, pending, blocked)
   639вҶ’- Progress badges (e.g., "3/5 met")
   640вҶ’
   641вҶ’**Live Updates**
   642вҶ’- File watcher on `.sequant/state.json`
   643вҶ’- SSE broadcasts changes to all connected browsers
   644вҶ’- Connection status indicator (green dot = live)
   645вҶ’
   646вҶ’**Summary Stats**
   647вҶ’- Total issues tracked
   648вҶ’- In Progress / QA Gate / Ready / Blocked counts
   649вҶ’- Grouped by status, sorted by last activity
   650вҶ’

   987вҶ’## What Makes Sequant Different
   988вҶ’
   989вҶ’| Feature | Without Sequant | With Sequant |
   990вҶ’|---------|-----------------|--------------|
   991вҶ’| **Planning** | "I'll figure it out as I go" | AC extracted, plan reviewed |
   992вҶ’| **Isolation** | All work in one branch | Worktree per issue |
   993вҶ’| **Quality** | "Looks good to me" | Automated checks, verdicts |
   994вҶ’| **State** | "Where was I?" | Full phase tracking |
   995вҶ’| **Learning** | Start fresh each time | Analytics on what works |
   996вҶ’

   998вҶ’
   999вҶ’## Philosophy
  1000вҶ’
  1001вҶ’**Local-first, always.** Your code stays on your machine.
  1002вҶ’No telemetry. No cloud dependencies (except GitHub, which
  1003вҶ’you're already using).
  1004вҶ’
  1005вҶ’**Phases, not chaos.** Breaking work into discrete phases
  1006вҶ’(plan, build, test, review) catches problems early and
  1007вҶ’ensures nothing gets skipped.
  1008вҶ’
  1009вҶ’**Quality gates, not gatekeeping.** Automated checks help
  1010вҶ’you ship better code faster. They're not there to slow you
  1011вҶ’down вҖ” they're there to catch what you'd miss at 2am.
  1012вҶ’
  1013вҶ’**Works with Claude Code, not against it.** Sequant enhances
  1014вҶ’Claude Code with structure. It doesn't replace your workflow
  1015вҶ’вҖ” it quantizes it.
  1016вҶ’
  1017вҶ’---
  1018вҶ’

1вҶ’# Merge Command
     2вҶ’
     3вҶ’**Quick Start:** Run batch-level integration checks on feature branches after `sequant run` completes. Catches cross-issue gaps that per-issue QA misses вҖ” merge conflicts, template mirroring gaps, residual patterns, and file overlaps вҖ” at zero AI cost.
     4вҶ’
     5вҶ’## Access
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’- **Command:** `npx sequant merge [issues...] [options]`
     8вҶ’- **Requirements:**
     9вҶ’  - Feature branches exist (pushed to remote or in local worktrees)
    10вҶ’  - GitHub CLI authenticated (`gh auth login`) for `--post` flag
    11вҶ’- **Relationship:** Runs AFTER `sequant run`, BEFORE `/merger`
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’```text
    14вҶ’sequant run 265 298 299 300   # implement
    15вҶ’sequant merge --check         # verify (this command)
    16вҶ’/merger 265 298 299 300       # merge
    17вҶ’```
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’## Usage
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’### Check a Completed Batch
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’```bash
    24вҶ’npx sequant merge 265 298 299 300 --check
    25вҶ’```
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’Runs Phase 1 deterministic checks: combined branch test, template mirroring, and file overlap detection.
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### Auto-Detect Issues from Last Run
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’```bash
    32вҶ’npx sequant merge --check
    33вҶ’```
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’Reads the most recent run log from `.sequant/logs/` and auto-detects which issues to check.
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’### Full Scan with Residual Pattern Detection
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’```bash
    40вҶ’npx sequant merge 265 298 299 300 --scan
    41вҶ’```
    42вҶ’
    43вҶ’Runs Phase 1 + Phase 2: adds residual pattern detection that finds instances of removed code patterns still present elsewhere in the codebase.
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’### Post Results to GitHub
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’```bash
    48вҶ’npx sequant merge 265 298 299 300 --check --post
    49вҶ’```
    50вҶ’
    51вҶ’Posts per-issue merge readiness reports as comments on each PR.
    52вҶ’
    53вҶ’## Options & Settings
    54вҶ’
    55вҶ’| Option | Description | Default |
    56вҶ’|--------|-------------|---------|
    57вҶ’| `--check` | Run Phase 1 deterministic checks | Default if no flag specified |
    58вҶ’| `--scan` | Run Phase 1 + Phase 2 residual pattern detection | - |
    59вҶ’| `--review` | Run Phase 1 + 2 + 3 AI briefing (stub) | - |
    60вҶ’| `--all` | Run all phases | - |
    61вҶ’| `--post` | Post report to GitHub as PR comments | - |
    62вҶ’| `--json` | Output as JSON (for scripting) | - |
    63вҶ’| `-v, --verbose` | Enable verbose output | - |
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’### Exit Codes
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’| Code | Meaning |
    68вҶ’|------|---------|
    69вҶ’| `0` | All checks pass (READY) |
    70вҶ’| `1` | Warnings found (NEEDS_ATTENTION) |
    71вҶ’| `2` | Failures found (BLOCKED) |
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’## Checks Performed
    74вҶ’
    75вҶ’### Phase 1: Deterministic Checks (`--check`)
    76вҶ’
    77вҶ’| Check | What It Does | Verdict |
    78вҶ’|-------|-------------|---------|
    79вҶ’| **Combined Branch Test** | Creates temp branch, merges all feature branches, runs `npm test && npm run build` | FAIL if merge conflict or test/build failure |
    80вҶ’| **Template Mirroring** | Verifies `.claude/skills/` changes have matching `templates/skills/` updates (and vice versa for `hooks/`) | WARN if unmirrored changes found |
    81вҶ’| **File Overlap Detection** | Flags files modified by multiple issues in the batch, classifies as additive vs conflicting | WARN if overlaps found |
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’### Phase 2: Residual Pattern Detection (`--scan`)
    84вҶ’
    85вҶ’| Check | What It Does | Verdict |
    86вҶ’|-------|-------------|---------|
    87вҶ’| **Residual Pattern Scan** | Extracts removed patterns from each PR's diff, searches codebase for remaining instances | WARN if residuals found |
    88вҶ’
    89вҶ’The residual scan uses literal string matching (minimum 8 characters) with `git grep`. It excludes files already modified by the PR, test files, and `node_modules`.
    90вҶ’
    91вҶ’### Phase 3: AI Briefing (`--review`)
    92вҶ’
    93вҶ’Not yet implemented. Returns a stub message directing you to use `--check` or `--scan`.
    94вҶ’
    95вҶ’## Common Workflows
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’### Standard Post-Run Verification
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’After `sequant run` completes a batch:
   100вҶ’
   101вҶ’```bash
   102вҶ’# 1. Run merge checks
   103вҶ’npx sequant merge --scan
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’# 2. Review the report
   106вҶ’#    READY       вҶ’ safe to merge
   107вҶ’#    NEEDS_ATTENTION вҶ’ review warnings
   108вҶ’#    BLOCKED     вҶ’ fix before merging
   109вҶ’
   110вҶ’# 3. Merge passing issues
   111вҶ’/merger 265 298 299 300
   112вҶ’```
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’### Scripted CI Integration
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’Use JSON output and exit codes for automation:
   117вҶ’
   118вҶ’```bash
   119вҶ’npx sequant merge --check --json > report.json
   120вҶ’exit_code=$?
   121вҶ’
   122вҶ’if [ $exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
   123вҶ’  echo "All clear to merge"
   124вҶ’elif [ $exit_code -eq 1 ]; then
   125вҶ’  echo "Warnings found вҖ” review report.json"
   126вҶ’else
   127вҶ’  echo "Blocked вҖ” fix issues before merging"
   128вҶ’fi
   129вҶ’```
   130вҶ’
   131вҶ’### Post Findings to PRs
   132вҶ’
   133вҶ’Share results with reviewers by posting to GitHub:
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’```bash
   136вҶ’npx sequant merge 265 298 299 300 --scan --post
   137вҶ’```
   138вҶ’
   139вҶ’Each PR receives a per-issue report showing its specific findings and the batch-level verdict.
   140вҶ’
   141вҶ’## Report Format
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’### Markdown Output (default)
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’```text
   146вҶ’# Merge Readiness Report
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’**Generated:** 2026-02-21T19:30:00.000Z
   149вҶ’**Batch Verdict:** NEEDS_ATTENTION
   150вҶ’
   151вҶ’## Per-Issue Verdicts
   152вҶ’
   153вҶ’| Issue | Title | Verdict |
   154вҶ’|-------|-------|---------|
   155вҶ’| #265 | Audit skill files | WARN |
   156вҶ’| #298 | Add test tautology | PASS |
   157вҶ’
   158вҶ’## Combined Branch Test
   159вҶ’- npm test passed on combined state
   160вҶ’- npm run build passed on combined state
   161вҶ’
   162вҶ’## Mirroring
   163вҶ’- Modified .claude/skills/qa/SKILL.md but not templates/skills/qa/SKILL.md
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’## Overlap Detection
   166вҶ’- src/commands/run.ts modified by issues #298, #299 (additive)
   167вҶ’
   168вҶ’## Summary
   169вҶ’- Errors: 0
   170вҶ’- Warnings: 2
   171вҶ’- Issues in batch: 4
   172вҶ’- Checks run: 3
   173вҶ’```
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’### JSON Output (`--json`)
   176вҶ’
   177вҶ’Returns structured JSON with all check results, per-issue verdicts, and the batch verdict. Useful for scripting and CI integration.
   178вҶ’
   179вҶ’## Verdicts
   180вҶ’
   181вҶ’### Per-Issue
   182вҶ’
   183вҶ’| Verdict | Meaning |
   184вҶ’|---------|---------|
   185вҶ’| **PASS** | No issues found for this issue |
   186вҶ’| **WARN** | Warnings found (mirroring gaps, residuals, overlaps) |
   187вҶ’| **FAIL** | Critical issues (merge conflicts, test/build failures) |
   188вҶ’
   189вҶ’### Batch-Level
   190вҶ’
   191вҶ’| Verdict | Meaning | Exit Code |
   192вҶ’|---------|---------|-----------|
   193вҶ’| **READY** | All issues pass, safe to merge | `0` |
   194вҶ’| **NEEDS_ATTENTION** | Warnings found, review before merging | `1` |
   195вҶ’| **BLOCKED** | Critical failures, fix before merging | `2` |
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’## Troubleshooting
   198вҶ’
   199вҶ’### "No run logs found"
   200вҶ’
   201вҶ’**Symptoms:** Error when running `sequant merge` without issue numbers.
   202вҶ’
   203вҶ’**Solution:** Either specify issues explicitly or run `sequant run` first to generate a run log:
   204вҶ’```bash
   205вҶ’# Explicit issues
   206вҶ’npx sequant merge 265 298 --check
   207вҶ’
   208вҶ’# Or run first
   209вҶ’npx sequant run 265 298
   210вҶ’npx sequant merge --check
   211вҶ’```
   212вҶ’
   213вҶ’### "No feature branches found"
   214вҶ’
   215вҶ’**Symptoms:** Error saying no branches found for specified issues.
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’**Solution:** Ensure feature branches exist вҖ” either pushed to remote or in local worktrees:
   218вҶ’```bash
   219вҶ’# Check remote branches
   220вҶ’git branch -r | grep feature/265
   221вҶ’
   222вҶ’# Check worktrees
   223вҶ’git worktree list
   224вҶ’```
   225вҶ’
   226вҶ’### Combined branch test reports merge conflict
   227вҶ’
   228вҶ’**Symptoms:** BLOCKED verdict due to merge conflict between feature branches.
   229вҶ’
   230вҶ’**Solution:** This means two feature branches modify the same file in incompatible ways. Options:
   231вҶ’1. Merge one branch first, then rebase the other
   232вҶ’2. Manually resolve the conflict before merging both
   233вҶ’3. If the conflict is in auto-generated files, it may resolve after merging one branch
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’### Mirroring warnings for intentional divergences
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’**Symptoms:** WARN for a `.claude/skills/` file that intentionally differs from `templates/skills/`.
   238вҶ’
   239вҶ’**Solution:** Mirroring warnings are advisory. If the divergence is intentional (e.g., project-specific paths), note it in your PR description and proceed with merge.
   240вҶ’
